doc_findings:
Lungs and pleura:
Linear atelectasis in the right middle lobe, lingular, and basal segments bilaterally.
Previously noted ground-glass nodules in the right lung have completely resolved.
A few perifissural nodule in the left lung measuring up to 3 mm (image 98), unchanged (likely a perifissural lymph node).
No pleural effusion.
The trachea and main bronchi are patent.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila:
No mass or lymphadenopathy.

Heart:
Cardiac chambers are enlarged (cardiothoracic ratio greater than 1/2).
No significant pericardial effusion.
Aortic valve calcification.
Moderate coronary calcification (more pronounced in the left anterior descending artery and in the proximal right coronary artery). The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

Great vessels:
Thoracic aorta is dilated up to 4.4 cm, with wall calcification.
Main pulmonary artery is dilated up to 4.0 cm.

Skeletal structures and chest wall:
Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with osteophyte formation.
Decreased density of vertebral bodies at the T11-L1 level to +123 HU.
Decreased height of the T7 vertebral body up to 25.5%.
Partially included in the scan range: C7-C6 transpedicular vertebral fixation.
Osteosclerotic lesion of the posterior segment of the left 2nd rib measuring up to 8 mm.

Upper abdomen:
Gallbladder stones measuring up to 5 mm, without gallbladder wall thickening.
doc_impression:
1. Previously noted ground-glass nodules in the right lung have completely resolved. No pulmonary nodules requiring further follow-up per 2017 Fleischner Society recommendations are identified.

2. Dilated main pulmonary artery (4.0 cm), which may suggest pulmonary hypertension.

3. Moderate coronary calcification. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

4. Cardiomegaly.

5. Aortic valve calcification.

6. Atherosclerosis of the thoracic aorta with dilation of the ascending aorta.

7. Decreased vertebral body density (CT signs of demineralization) at the T11-L1 level, decreased height of the T7 vertebral body, C7-C6 transpedicular fixation (partially included in the scan range in the lower cervical spine), thoracic spondylosis. Osteosclerotic lesion of the left 2nd rib (Bone-RADS 1).

8. Gallbladder stones.
val_findings:
Lungs and pleura: Multiple ground-glass nodules in the right lung noted on December 22, 2025, have completely resolved. A few perifissural nodules in the left lung measure up to 3 mm (image 98), unchanged (likely a perifissural lymph node), require no follow-up. Multiple small pleural calcifications on both sides in the lower parts, unchanged. Linear atelectasis in the right middle lobe, lingular, and basal segments bilaterally. Gravitational changes. No pleural effusion. The trachea and main bronchi are patent.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No mass or lymphadenopathy.

Heart: The heart is enlarged due to increased size of the chambers. Multiple fatty inclusions of the myocardium of the ventricles may correspond to the consequence of ischemic heart disease. Pericardial effusion with a thickness of 6 mm (previously in a smaller volume). Mild aortic and mitral valve calcifications. Coronary artery calcifications are identified in the left main, left anterior descending, left circumflex, and right coronary arteries, qualitatively assessed as moderate.

Great vessels: Thoracic aorta is dilated up to 4.4 cm, with moderate wall calcification, unchanged. Main pulmonary artery is dilated up to 3.7 cm, unchanged.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with osteophyte formation. Decreased density of vertebral bodies at the T11-L1 level to +123 HU, decreased height of the T7 vertebral body up to 25.5% (CT signs of demineralization). Partially included in the scan range: C7-C6 transpedicular vertebral fixation. Osteosclerotic lesion of the posterior segment of the left 2nd rib measuring up to 8 mm, unchanged compared to Chest CT on December 22, 2025.

Upper abdomen: Gallbladder stones measuring up to 5 mm, without gallbladder wall thickening. Multiple small hypodense liver lesions up to 5 mm, unchanged. ACR White Paper recommendations (Gore, et al. J Am Coll Radiol 2017;14:1429-1437) suggest the following: For patients with low risk of having a malignant hepatic lesion, no need for further follow-up. For patients with high risk of having a malignant hepatic lesion, follow-up MRI in 3 to 6 months is recommended for further evaluation.
val_impression:
1. Multiple ground-glass nodules in the right lung noted on December 22, 2025, have completely resolved, were likely inflammatory/infectious changes. No pulmonary nodules requiring further follow-up per 2017 Fleischner Society recommendations are identified.

2. Coronary artery calcifications are identified in the left main, left anterior descending, left circumflex, and right coronary arteries, qualitatively assessed as moderate. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

3. Dilated main pulmonary artery (3.7 cm), which may suggest pulmonary hypertension.

4. Enlargement of the heart chambers. Small-volume pericardial effusion. Mild aortic and mitral valve calcifications. Atherosclerosis of the thoracic aorta with dilation of the ascending aorta up to 4.4 cm.

5. Cholelithiasis.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.
val_protocol:
INDICATION: Solitary pulmonary nodule. Shortness of breath for 2 years.

COMPARISON: Chest CT on December 22, 2025, September 15, 2025.

TECHNIQUE: Scans were obtained through the thorax without IV contrast, to include contiguous axial images with bone and soft tissue algorithm. Thin slice axial reformat and coronal reformat images were included. Artificial Intelligence assessment was performed separately on the Nodule Prediction workstation. CT scan done according to ALARA (As Low As Reasonably Achievable).

RADIATION DOSE: CTDI volume 17.28 mGy; total DLP 637.49 mGy-cm.

Prior known CT or cardiac nuclear medicine studies performed in the last 12 months: 2.

doc_findings:
FINDINGS:

Lungs and pleura: Small areas of apical fibrosis in the upper lobes of both lungs. No nodules requiring follow-up are identified. The lungs are unremarkable. No pleural effusion. No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No masses or lymphadenopathy are identified.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No clinically significant pericardial effusion. Moderate coronary calcification.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated. Coronary calcifications are present: Agatston score 51, CAC-DRS A1. Paracardiac fat volume is within normal limits. Pericardial fluid volume is within normal limits. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: No rib fractures are identified. Vertebral body heights are preserved. Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with marginal osteophytes from T1 to L1.

Upper abdomen: Cholecystectomy.

Thyroid gland: Diffusely heterogeneous, with calcific inclusions.
doc_impression:
1. Small linear changes in the upper lobes of both lungs, likely postinflammatory. These changes do not require follow-up.

2. Moderate coronary calcification.

3. Atherosclerotic changes of the thoracic aorta.

4. Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine.

5. Cholecystectomy.

6. Diffuse thyroid changes with calcifications. Thyroid ultrasound is recommended (per Managing Incidental Thyroid Nodules Detected on Imaging: ACR, 2014).
val_findings:
Lungs and pleura: Bilateral apical scarring. No nodules requiring follow-up are identified. Gravitational changes in the dorsal regions, clinically insignificant. No signs of current pneumonia. No pleural effusion. Mild bronchial wall thickening.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No masses or lymphadenopathy are identified.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. Mild enlargement of the left ventricle. No clinically significant pericardial effusion. Coronary artery calcifications are identified in the left main, left anterior descending, and right coronary arteries, qualitatively assessed as moderate.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Mild degenerative changes of the thoracic spine.

Upper abdomen: Cholecystectomy.

Thyroid gland: Heterogeneous thyroid gland, consistent with ultrasound data from October 14, 2025, requires no follow-up.
val_impression:
1. Bilateral apical scarring consistent with chest X-ray report on February 10, 2026. Gravitational changes in the dorsal regions, clinically insignificant. No nodules requiring follow-up are identified. No signs of current pneumonia.

2. Coronary artery calcifications are identified in the left main, left anterior descending, and right coronary arteries, qualitatively assessed as moderate. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for a comprehensive cardiovascular risk assessment or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries. Note: Recommendation applies unless the patient has known CAD, prior CABG/stent, trauma indications, or intraoperative CTs.
Mild enlargement of the left ventricle. Atherosclerotic changes of the thoracic aorta.



DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.

doc_findings:
Lungs and pleura: No nodules requiring follow-up are identified. Lungs are unremarkable.  
No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No mass lesions or lymphadenopathy are identified.

Heart: No cardiomegaly is identified. No significant pericardial effusion. No coronary calcification is identified.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Unremarkable.

Upper abdomen: Decreased liver density to +39 HU (previously +28 HU). Bariatric gastric surgery. Metallic clips are visualized in the region of the gastric fundus and the cardioesophageal junction.
doc_impression:
1. No nodules requiring follow-up per Fleischner Society recommendations are identified.

2. Diffuse decreased density of the liver parenchyma (previously more pronounced, to +28 HU; currently to +39 HU), which may correspond to hepatic steatosis with positive interval change. Clinical correlation is recommended.

3. Bariatric gastric surgery.
val_findings:
Lungs and pleura: In the lung parenchyma, solid nodules are identified, in series 7 presented as follows:
In the upper right lobe:
- 3 mm nodule on image 60.
In the middle right lobe:
- 2 mm nodule on image 95.
In the upper left lobe:
- 3 mm nodule on image 72.
In the lower left lobe:
- 2 mm calcified nodule on image 44, healed granuloma, requires no follow-up.
A few areas of air trapping, clinically insignificant. No consolidation, atelectasis, emphysema, or honeycombing. No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No mass or lymphadenopathy. Residual thymus tissue.

Heart: No cardiomegaly is identified. No significant pericardial effusion. No coronary calcification is identified.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Ossifications along the periphery of the humeral heads are present (the shoulder joints are not fully included in the scanning area), which is consistent with the shoulders radiograph 2/20/2026.

Upper abdomen: Decreased liver density to +39 HU (previously +28 HU), interval improvement of the hepatic steatosis. Gastric surgery, metallic clips are visualized in the region of the gastric fundus and the cardioesophageal junction.
val_impression:
1. No consolidation, atelectasis, emphysema, or honeycombing in both lungs. A few nodules up to 3 mm in both lungs. ACR Fleischner Society recommendations (MacMahon, et al. Radiology 2017; 284(1): 228-43) suggest the following:
- For patients with low risk of lung cancer, no need for further follow-up.
- For patients with high risk of lung cancer, generally no need for follow-up. An optional follow-up chest CT at 12 months could be performed if there is high index of suspicion. If unchanged, no further follow-up is necessary.

2. No coronary calcification is identified.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.

doc_findings:
Lungs and pleura: Micro-nodules in the right upper lobe measuring up to 2 mm in diameter. Mild ground-glass opacities in both lungs, interlobular septal thickening with formation of a mosaic attenuation pattern. Discoid atelectasis in the anterior and lung bases of the right lung and in the anterior portions of the left lung. Bronchial walls are irregularly thickened.  
No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No mass and no lymphadenopathy.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No significant pericardial effusion. Coronary calcification, qualitatively assessed as mild. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries. Aortic valve calcification. Left coronary artery stenting.

Great vessels: Thoracic aorta is not dilated. Calcified atherosclerotic plaques in the wall of the thoracic aorta. S-shaped tortuosity of the thoracic aorta. Dilated main pulmonary artery measuring up to 30 mm.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. Decreased density of vertebral bodies: T11 - 106 HU, T12 - 91 HU. Decreased height of the L1 vertebral body by 25%.

Upper abdomen: Hiatal hernia measuring 3.1 × 3.4 × 4.1 cm, hernia contents include part of the stomach.
doc_impression:
1. Micro-nodules in the right upper lobe. No nodules requiring follow-up per Fleischner Society recommendations are identified.

2. Discoid atelectasis in both lungs.

3. Coronary calcification, qualitatively assessed as mild. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries. Aortic valve calcification.

4. Dilated main pulmonary artery (3.0 cm), which may suggest pulmonary hypertension.

5. S-shaped tortuosity of the thoracic aorta.

6. Decreased vertebral body density (CT signs of demineralization).

7. Decreased height of the L1 vertebral body.

8. Hiatal hernia.
val_findings:
Lungs and pleura: Diffuse mosaic attenuation pattern in both lungs, interlobular septal thickening in the apices of the lungs. Micro-nodules in the right upper lobe measure up to 2 mm in diameter. Discoid atelectasis in the right middle lobe, left lingula, lung bases of the right lung. No signs of interstitial disease (honeycombing, traction bronchiectasis, reticulations) were detected. Mild bronchial wall thickening. No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No mass and no lymphadenopathy. Hiatal hernia measuring 3.1 × 3.4 × 4.1 cm, hernia contents include stomach cardia.

Heart: The heart is slightly enlarged due to increased size of the chambers. No significant pericardial effusion. Coronary artery calcifications are identified in the left main, left anterior descending, left circumflex arteries, qualitatively assessed as moderate. Aortic and mitral valve calcifications.

Great vessels: Thoracic aorta is not dilated. Calcified atherosclerotic plaques in the wall of the thoracic aorta. S-shaped tortuosity of the thoracic aorta. Dilated main pulmonary artery measures up to 3.0 cm.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. Decreased density of vertebral bodies: T11 - 106 HU, T12 - 91 HU. Hemangioma L1 measures up to 2.0 cm, no follow-up is required.

Upper abdomen: The common hepatic and common bile ducts are dilated, measuring up to 1.0 cm. The gallbladder is contracted. No calculi are identified within the visualized ductal lumina.

Thyroid: In the right lobe there is a hypodense nodule up to 4 mm. ACR White Paper recommendations (Hoang, et al. J Am Coll Radiol 2015;12:143-150) suggest the following: No need for further follow-up.
val_impression:
1. Diffuse mosaic attenuation pattern in both lungs may be consistent with obliterative small airways disease or vascular disease, including pulmonary hypertension. No signs of interstitial disease were detected. Micronodules in the right upper lobe. ACR Fleischner Society recommendations (MacMahon, et al. Radiology 2017; 284(1): 228-43) suggest the following:
- For patients with low risk of lung cancer, no need for further follow-up.
- For patients with high risk of lung cancer, generally no need for follow-up. An optional follow-up chest CT at 12 months could be performed if there is high index of suspicion. If unchanged, no further follow-up is necessary.

2. Coronary calcification, qualitatively assessed as moderate. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries. Note: Recommendation applies unless the patient has known CAD, prior CABG/stent, trauma indications or intraoperative CTs.

3. Dilated main pulmonary artery (3.0 cm), which may suggest pulmonary hypertension.

4. Aortic and mitral valve calcifications.

5. Decreased vertebral body density (CT signs of demineralization).

6. Hiatal hernia.

7. Common hepatic and common bile duct dilatation up to 1.0 cm with a contracted gallbladder. Consider an abdominal ultrasound for further evaluation of findings.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.

doc_findings:
Lungs and pleura: A few calcifications in the left upper lobe, measuring 6 mm on image 50; measuring 4 mm. No nodules requiring follow-up are identified. Aeration is unevenly increased due to multiple areas of mixed emphysema with a total extent of involvement of 17%. Subsegmental atelectasis in the upper and lower lobes of both lungs. No pleural effusion. Bochdalek hernias, measuring 5.4 × 2.0 cm on the right and 3.8 × 1.6 cm on the left.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No mass lesions or lymphadenopathy are identified.

Heart: No cardiomegaly is identified. No clinically significant pericardial effusion. Coronary calcium is not identified. Calcifications are present in the walls of the coronary arteries: Agatston score 47, CAC-DRS A1. Increased paracardiac fat volume is identified: total volume 218 mL, epicardial 151 mL, pericardial 67 mL.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Decreased density of vertebral bodies: T11 - 134 HU, T12 - 122 HU, L1 - 112 HU. Severe degenerative-dystrophic changes of the spine with formation of marginal osteophytes in the C6-L2 segment.

Upper abdomen: Unremarkable.
doc_impression:
1. CT findings of mixed pulmonary emphysema (total extent of involvement about 17%). Pulmonary emphysema on CT is an independent risk factor for lung cancer. Consider enrollment in a low-dose CT (LDCT) lung cancer screening program if the patient meets eligibility criteria.

2. A few calcified nodules in the left upper lobe, consistent with postinflammatory changes. These changes do not require follow-up.

3. Subsegmental atelectasis in the upper and lower lobes of both lungs.

4. Mild coronary calcification (Agatston 47, CAC-DRS A1). The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

5. Bilateral Bochdalek hernias.

6. Decreased vertebral body density (CT signs of demineralization). Clinical correlation is recommended and consideration of bone densitometry.

7. Severe degenerative-dystrophic changes of the spine.
val_findings:
Lungs and pleura: Severe confluent centrilobular emphysema. In the upper lobe of the left lung (series 5, images 47-52), calcified nodules measure 4 mm and 3 mm. On the right, the interlobar perifissural nodule measures up to 3 mm, lymph node, requires no follow-up. In the right lower lobe, a subpleural nodule measures 5 mm (image 179). In the left lower lobe, a subpleural nodule measures 3 mm (image 196). Discoid atelectasis in lung bases, middle lobe and lingular segments. No pleural effusion. Bochdalek hernias, measuring 5.4 × 2.0 cm on the right and 3.8 × 1.6 cm on the left.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No mass lesions or lymphadenopathy are identified.

Heart: No cardiomegaly is identified. No clinically significant pericardial effusion. Coronary artery calcifications are identified in the left main, left anterior descending, left circumflex, and right coronary arteries, qualitatively assessed as moderate.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated, with moderate wall calcifications. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Decreased density of vertebral bodies: T11 - 134 HU, T12 - 122 HU, L1 - 112 HU. Moderate degenerative changes of the spine.

Upper abdomen: Unremarkable.
val_impression:
1. Calcified nodules in the left upper lobe measure up to 4 mm, healed granulomas, require no follow-up. Subpleural nodules measure up to 5 mm in lower lobes. ACR Fleischner Society recommendations (MacMahon, et al. Radiology 2017; 284(1): 228-43) suggest the following:
- For patients with low risk of lung cancer, no need for further follow-up.
- For patients with high risk of lung cancer, generally no need for follow-up. An optional follow-up chest CT at 12 months could be performed if there is high index of suspicion. If unchanged, no further follow-up is necessary.

2. Severe confluent centrilobular emphysema. Pulmonary emphysema on CT is an independent risk factor for lung cancer. Consider enrollment in a low-dose CT (LDCT) lung cancer screening program if the patient meets eligibility criteria; today's CT may serve as a baseline for future screening. Note: Recommendation applies unless the patient has a history of lung cancer, known pulmonary nodules or lung mass, receives routine chest CTs/screening, or is receiving palliative care.

3. Coronary artery calcifications are identified in the left main, left anterior descending, left circumflex, and right coronary arteries, qualitatively assessed as moderate. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for a comprehensive cardiovascular risk assessment or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries. Note: Recommendation applies unless the patient has known CAD, prior CABG/stent, trauma indications or intraoperative CTs.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.
